# carts package

The `carts` R package is a simulation tool for exploring various
estimators within a clinical trial context under differing assumptions.
The package provides a user-friendly interface for defining a clinical
trial object with different choices for its design, patient and endpoint
distributions, and treatment effect estimators. Once a clinical trial
object is specified, the package provides the functionality for
estimating the power with Monte Carlo simulations.

## Example

Here we emulate a simple parallel trial design with both observed and
unobserved covariates. The treatment effect is estimated using an
estimator based on the efficient influence function (EIF) where we
adjust for the observed covariate x.

The necessary sample-size to achieve 90% power for a one-sided
superiority test is estimated using a variation of a bisection and a
Robbins-Monro stochastic approximation algorithm with parallelized
computations

``` R
library("carts")

## Loading required package: lava

suppressMessages(library("data.table"))
future::plan(future::multicore)
## progressr::handlers(global = TRUE)
## progressr::handlers(progressr::handler_cli)

## Covariates at baseline
x0 <- function(n, pa = 0.5, gamma.var = 0.7, ...) {
  data.table(
    a = rbinom(n, 1, pa), ## Treatment
    x = rnorm(n), ## Obs.
    z = log(rgamma(n, shape = 1 / gamma.var, rate = 1 / gamma.var)) ## Unobs.
  )
}
## Outcome model
outcome <- function(data, b = c(log(2.5), log(0.38)), ...) {
  X <- model.matrix(~ 1 + a, data)
  rate <- exp(X %*% b + with(data, x + z))
  data.table(y = rpois(length(rate), rate))
}

qmodel  <- targeted::learner_glm(y ~ a * x, family = poisson)
m <- Trial$new(
  covariates = x0,
  outcome = outcome,
  estimators = list(adj = est_adj(qmodel))
  )

## Sample-size estimation via Stochastic Approximation
set.seed(42)
e <- m$estimate_samplesize(R = 1000)
print(e)

## ── Estimated sample-size to reach 90% power ── 
## 
## n = 97 (actual estimated power≈91.88%)
```

## Installation

The package can be installed on all unix systems from the command line
via `make install`. Already installed R packages that are dependencies
of `carts` will not be upgrade with `make install`. Instead, use the
`make upgrade` rule to upgrade all dependencies, followed by the
installation of the package.

## Project organization

We use the `dev` branch for development and the `main` branch for stable
releases. All releases follow [semantic
versioning](https://semver.org/), are
[tagged](https://github.com/NovoNordisk-OpenSource/carts/tags) and
notable changes are reported in the
[NEWS.md](https://github.com/NovoNordisk-OpenSource/carts/blob/main/NEWS.md)
file.

## I Have a Question / I Want to Report a Bug

If you want to ask questions, require help or clarification, or report a
bug, we recommend to either contact a maintainer directly or the
following:

- Open an
  [Issue](https://github.com/NovoNordisk-OpenSource/carts/issues).
- Provide as much context as you can about what you’re running into.
- Provide project and platform versions, depending on what seems
  relevant.

We will then take care of the issue as soon as possible.

## Maintainers

> Benedikt Sommer (<benediktsommer92@gmail.com>)  
> Klaus Kähler Holst (<klaus@holst.it>)

# Package index

## All functions

- [`Trial`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/Trial.md)
  : R6 class for power and sample-size calculations for a clinical trial
- [`aggrsurv()`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/aggrsurv.md)
  : Aggregate data in counting process format
- [`` `append<-`( ``*`<list>`*`)`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/append-set-.list.md)
  : Assignment function to append values to existing list
- [`bisection()`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/bisection.md)
  : Root finding by bisection
- [`covar_add()`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/covar_add.md)
  : Add additional covariates to existing list of covariates
- [`covar_bootstrap()`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/covar_bootstrap.md)
  : Sample from empirical distribution of covariate data
- [`covar_join()`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/covar_join.md)
  : Add additional covariates to existing covariate random generator
- [`covar_loggamma()`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/covar_loggamma.md)
  : Simulate from a log gamma-gaussian copula distribution
- [`covar_normal()`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/covar_normal.md)
  : Simulate from multivariate normal distribution
- [`derive_covar_distribution()`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/derive_covar_distribution.md)
  : Derive covariate distribution from covariate data type
- [`est_adj()`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/est_adj.md)
  : Construct estimator for the treatment effect in RCT based on
  covariate adjustment
- [`est_glm()`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/est_glm.md)
  : Construct estimator for the treatment effect in RCT
- [`est_phreg()`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/est_phreg.md)
  : Marginal Cox proportional hazards model for the treatment effect in
  RCT
- [`estimate_covar_model_full_cond()`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/estimate_covar_model_full_cond.md)
  : Full conditional covariate simulation model
- [`get_factor_levels()`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/get_factor_levels.md)
  : Get levels for factor columns in data.table
- [`optim_sa()`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/optim_sa.md)
  : Root solver by Stochastic Approximation
- [`outcome_binary()`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/outcome_binary.md)
  : Simulate from binary model given covariates
- [`outcome_continuous()`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/outcome_continuous.md)
  : Simulate from continuous outcome model given covariates
- [`outcome_count()`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/outcome_count.md)
  : Simulate from count model given covariates
- [`outcome_phreg()`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/outcome_phreg.md)
  : Outcome model for time-to-event end-points (proportional hazards)
- [`outcome_recurrent()`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/outcome_recurrent.md)
  : EXPERIMENTAL: Outcome model for recurrent events with terminal
  events end-points
- [`rmvn()`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/rmvn.md)
  : Multivariate normal distribution function
- [`rnb()`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/rnb.md)
  : Simulate from a negative binomial distribution
- [`sample_covar_parametric_model()`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/sample_covar_parametric_model.md)
  : Sample from an estimated parametric covariate model
- [`setargs()`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/setargs.md)
  : Set default arguments of a function
- [`trial.estimates-class`](https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/carts/reference/trial.estimates-class.md)
  : trial.estimates class object
